themes.name: US Stocks Trading Issues
themes.description: Users are reporting significant problems with trading US stocks, including transaction failures, fund deposit/withdrawal delays, and lack of essential features like order depth.

themes.name: Customer Support & Issue Resolution
themes.description: A recurring theme is the dissatisfaction with customer support, citing unresponsiveness, generic replies, unresolved issues, and delays in problem resolution.

themes.name: App Functionality & User Experience (UX/UI)
themes.description: Users are experiencing bugs, confusing interfaces, missing features, and unwanted changes in app updates, impacting their overall experience.

themes.name: Loan & Payment Issues
themes.description: Concerns are raised about loan interest rates, payment gateway failures, and aggressive loan reminder calls.

themes.name: Feature Requests & Improvements
themes.description: Users are suggesting new features and improvements, such as adding more banks, insurance aggregation, ETF options, and advanced trading tools.

quotes.text: The app is not functioning as expected. For US stocks even after plaving order and adding otp it says somwthing went wrong even though the transaction went thru.

Don't invest in US stocks via INDMoney. Very bad customer support. I initiated transaction for 25K, but it never reached by trading account. I am still waiting for it to be credited from more than 3 weeks.
The old interface is too good for us. The new update interface is very complicated.

weekly_pulse.note: User feedback highlights critical issues with US stocks trading, including transaction failures and significant delays in fund processing. Customer support remains a major pain point, with users reporting unresponsiveness and unresolved issues. The app's UI/UX is also a concern, with many finding it confusing and requesting simplification. While some users appreciate the app's core functionality and beginner-friendliness, these persistent issues are impacting overall satisfaction and driving users to competitors. Addressing US stock trading reliability and improving customer support responsiveness are paramount.
actions: Investigate and resolve all reported US stock transaction failures and fund processing delays, providing clear communication on TATs and root causes.
Implement a tiered customer support system to ensure faster and more effective resolution of critical issues, with clear escalation paths.
Conduct a UX audit focused on simplifying the app's interface, particularly for trading functionalities, and address user feedback on the new update's complexity.

fee_scenario: Mutual Fund Exit Load on INDmoney Platform
explanation_bullets: An exit load is a charge levied by a mutual fund house when an investor redeems their units before a specified period.
The exit load percentage and the holding period for its applicability are determined by the respective mutual fund scheme, not by the INDmoney platform.
INDmoney displays the exit load details for each mutual fund scheme, including the applicable percentage and the tenure, within the app.
If an investor redeems units of a mutual fund scheme before the exit load period expires, the calculated exit load amount will be deducted from the redemption proceeds.
The exit load is a fee charged by the mutual fund Asset Management Company (AMC) and is not a fee charged by INDmoney for facilitating the transaction.
